notes: Git-anchored evals against the REAL GymApp project — no synthetic fixtures. Each eval is a case spec under rpiv/evals/cases/deep-review/ with a pinned worktree (detached at a fixed sha under C:/Users/pedro/Documents/GymApp-evals/), an optional planted patch (saved in cases/deep-review/patches/), and EXECUTABLE assertions graded by rpiv/evals/harness/grade-artifact.mjs (citation resolvability, verbatim-quote greppability, validate-artifact.mjs, recall of planted/known defects, blocker ceilings). Run one: node rpiv/evals/harness/run-eval.mjs --case rpiv/evals/cases/deep-review/DR-1.json --rundir <workspace/iteration-N/DR-1/with_skill>. Design + contamination controls: rpiv/evals/README.md.

evals.name: planted-contract-divergence
evals.prompt: /rpiv:deep-review my working changes before I commit — small cleanup in the adaptive validation path.
evals.expected_output: A review artifact that flags the inlined load-cap formula in lib/generation/adaptive-context/validation.ts:~311 (operator-precedence divergence from load-cap.ts) as a blocker, with resolvable citations and a verification pass.
assertions: grade-artifact: validate-artifact.mjs passes on the produced artifact
grade-artifact: >=95% of file:line citations resolve in the worktree; verbatim quotes match cited lines
grade-artifact: recall planted-cap-bug — validation.ts cited within ±12 of line 311
grade-artifact: frontmatter blockers_count >= 1 (severity calibration: a silent 10x cap error is a blocker)

evals.name: style-diff-discrimination
evals.prompt: /rpiv:deep-review commit e80409c — an automated sqlfluff style pass over the SQL migrations; want to be sure it changed nothing behavioral.
evals.expected_output: The 47-file style diff hides exactly ONE real breakage (atlas.sum checksums went stale — confirmed live by `atlas migrate validate`). Correct review: exactly that one blocker, nothing invented on the behavior-preserving SQL churn.
assertions: grade-artifact: >=95% citations resolve; verbatim quotes match
grade-artifact: mentions atlas.sum + the checksum/hash staleness (the one true blocker)
grade-artifact: frontmatter blockers_count == 1 (finds the real one, invents none)

evals.name: unwired-variant-registration
evals.prompt: /rpiv:deep-review my working changes — gave the final strength intensification week its own peak narrative key.
evals.expected_output: The review chases the new narrativeKey value into its consumers and flags that cycle-narrative.ts getCyclePhaseFocusSuffix has no case for it (the default: silently swallows it). The consumer file is NOT in the diff.
assertions: grade-artifact: >=95% citations resolve; verbatim quotes match
grade-artifact: recall unwired-narrative — lib/generation/cycle-narrative.ts is cited (a file outside the diff)
